message:
perf(ready): use targeted SQL existence query for empty-list message

The empty-ready-list helper previously called list_issues with a Limit(1)
filter just to check whether the project had any non-closed work. That
path materializes a full Issue row (id resolution, JSON deserialization
of labels/dependencies, source-of-truth metadata, etc.) just to ask a
boolean question, and runs through the standard list pipeline including
sort and visibility joins.

Replaces it with a new SqliteStorage::has_active_issues() method that
issues a direct existence query:

    SELECT 1 FROM issues
    WHERE status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone')
      AND (is_template = 0 OR is_template IS NULL)
    LIMIT 1

Mirrors the visibility used by the default ready/list views (excludes
closed and tombstone rows, ignores template issues) so the resulting
"all blocked vs nothing exists" diagnostic matches what the user would
see with `br ready`. The LIMIT 1 lets SQLite stop at the first match.

ready.rs is updated to call has_active_issues() and drops the now-unused
ListFilters import.
